course_title: Music on Screen: Movies, TV and Games

learning_outcomes.description: Appreciate and more fully understand film and video game music and its cultural and industrial implications.
Access cultural production and media analytically and critically.

Hear, identify, and discuss the musical characteristics and sonic production techniques that are employed in film and video games, and understand their methods of creation both technically and artistically.
Unpack the relationship between sonic, cultural and narrative tropes presented in film, television and games.

assessments.due_date:
24/08/2026

21/09/2026

26/10/2026

The marks from your two best attempts out of the three tests will count towards your final mark. This means that a student can miss one test and still achieve the maximum possible marks for the In-Class Online Multiple-Choice Tests assessment component (30%). You must be present from the commencement of your tutorial during test weeks. Late arrivals without a valid explanation will not be permitted to sit the test.
assessments.task_description:
You must complete three in-class online tests, held during tutorials in weeks 5, 9 and 13.

Each test consists of 30 multiple-choice questions based on the course readings: the week 5 test covers weeks 2, 3, 4 & 5; the week 9 test covers weeks 6, 7, 8 & 9; the week 13 test covers weeks 10, 11 & 13, plus 10 course revision questions.

You will be permitted to consult any notes you have made from the weekly readings, but no other documents or web pages may be viewed on devices during the test.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submitted in class via Blackboard Assessment section.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Write a close textual analysis of a brief (approx. five minutes) music sequence from a film or television programme of your choice. The essay should take the form of an examination of the narrative relationship between music and image in your chosen clip, and highlight pertinent theoretical or industrial connections relevant to the course, placing the chosen sequence within a researched scholarly framework.

A detailed criteria sheet will be available in the Blackboard Assessment section.
assessments.submission_guidelines: To be submitted in the Blackboard Assessment section via Turnitin.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Present a focused theoretical research essay based on one of the following topics:

·    silent film accompaniment

·    the introduction of film sound

·    industrial and cultural implications of the classical Hollywood score

·    music and film genre

·    alternatives to the classical score

·    jazz and film

·    art film or world cinema scores

·    rock movies since the 1950s

·    the rock compilation score

·    music documentary

·    film music and the auteur director

·    the film musical

·    video game music

·    the online relationship of music and visuals

 

Your essay should demonstrate an ability to define and research relevant and specific areas of film music studies. Research should take into account existing scholarly studies of the interrelations between music and visual media, and attempt to place representative texts within an established scholarly framework. Detailed analyses of selected aspects of musical soundtracks from specific films, television programs or games should be provided where appropriate in order to illustrate the relationship between music and image, or highlight pertinent theoretical or industrial connections. 

A detailed criteria sheet will be available in the Blackboard Assessment section.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 1 grade for each 24 hour period from time submission is due will apply for up to 7 days. After 7 days you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

Online Lectures

learning_activities.topic:
Tutorials

You attend a weekly Tutorial which provides you with opportunities to discuss lectures and readings and prepare your assignments.

During the tutorial you must also complete the weekly online test and present your question for discussion.


Note: There is no tutorial or test during week eight.

learning_activities.topic:
Weekly Readings

You will engage with a range of set readings before the commencement of lectures and tutorials each week. These are detailed in the Course List available via the UQ Library and Blackboard.


The lectures will explain concepts from this preparatory material, and expand your knowledge of concepts with examples and discussion.
